steps.title: Use the square shape
steps.content: The expression $(x+15)^2$ is always at least $0$. It equals $0$ at exactly one value of $x$, but it equals any positive constant at two values of $x$.

steps.title: Test the constants
steps.content:
A negative constant gives no real solution, $0$ gives one real solution, and a positive constant gives two real solutions. Only
$$(x+15)^2=0$$has exactly one distinct real solution. The answer is A.
